mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals weakening consumer spending capacity and deteriorating corporate revenue growth, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to yield positive 60-day forward returns in at least 65% of overlapping quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory shortages, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 40% of months following a 0.5% monthly decline in INDPRO during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 235 reflect entrenched inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns fail to materialize in at least 55% of months following a month-over-month increase in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production levels above 102 during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and rising input costs, which erode profit margins and trigger negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where INDPRO exceeds 102 during late-cycle phases.
